response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with breadth across web, mobile, and some embedded/systems work, but the role requires deep hardware performance architecture expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, and hardware-software co-design — which is absent from the resume. Low-latency DLang services and C/C++ work on a measurement IDE are the closest touchpoints, but they fall well short of silicon-level performance modeling, GPU/SIMD architecture, or deep-learning workload analysis expected at the principal level for this position.
strengths: Long, progressive engineering career with staff-level experience and strong software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang at millions of requests/sec)
Some C/C++ systems experience and recent exposure to AI tooling (MCP, agentic workflows)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or performance-modeling experience — the core of the role
No deep-learning training/inference optimization, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or accelerator workload background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not hardware-software co-design or system architecture at the silicon level

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator / deep-learning workload optimization background
Strong systems programming and low-latency optimization skills
